front: Play: Count di Nolli pays four valets to serve the protagonist for eleven years in a house modeled on a residence at Goslar
Play: the title character stabs Tito Belcredi (mistaken for Peter Damian) after a doctor tries to 'cure' him by replacing two portraits
Play: a portrait of Matilda dressed as a famous countess hangs in the protagonist's 'throne room'; he develops a taste for 'playing the madman' after twenty years
Novel: the title character uses Monte Carlo winnings to pose as Adriano Meis after being declared dead when his family mistakes another corpse for him
Novel: the librarian Don Eligio encourages the title character to write his story; the title character complains about Copernicus
Play: Laudisi investigates conflicting claims by Lady Frola and Mr. Ponza about Ponza's wife; looks in mirror asking 'Who is insane among us?'
back: Right You Are (If You Think So) (Luigi Pirandello)
work: Right You Are (If You Think So)

front: Play: a one-act dialogue between a man who missed his train and a man dying of facial epithelioma; the first TV drama produced by the BBC in 1930
back: The Man with a Flower in His Mouth (Luigi Pirandello)

front: Play: Doctor Hinkfuss fails to provide a script; actors improvise a staging of the novella Leonora, Addio!; Hinkfuss apologizes at end
Author: Dario Fo was the first Italian to win the Nobel Prize in Literature since this playwright
Author: this playwright's essay 'On Humor' examined the comic relief Don Abbondio provides in Manzoni's The Betrothed
